mechanism: All beneficial action is treated as one psychological mechanism
possible_dampener: Distinguish helping altruistic motive cooperation sacrifice and care

mechanism: Mixed motives are treated as moral failure
possible_dampener: Assess whether other-oriented concern is genuinely present and causally important

mechanism: Empathic concern fails to become action under cost ambiguity or diffusion
possible_dampener: Clarify responsibility reduce cost and create feasible response channels

mechanism: Concern favors familiar visible or similar others
possible_dampener: Widen moral recognition through testimony contact and justice norms
Test action under low visibility and recipient-centered conditions

mechanism: Support is limited to those who can repay
possible_dampener: Include dependence vulnerability and nonreciprocal care obligations

mechanism: Repeated need and weak support exhaust caregiving persistence
possible_dampener: Provide rest staffing shared responsibility and institutional support

mechanism: Institutions praise care while denying conditions for care
possible_dampener: Align workload metrics resources incentives and accountability

mechanism: Helping overrides recipient agency or dignity
possible_dampener: Use consent participation listening and respect for agency

mechanism: Everyone assumes someone else will help
possible_dampener: Clarify ownership roles escalation and follow-up
